q: When a capacitor is connected to a battery, the two conductors have charges of equal magnitude and opposite signs.
The magnitude of the charge on each conductor, $Q$, is called the ___ of the capacitor.

The capacitance of a capacitor is defined as the ratio of the charge to the potential difference of the capacitor.
The SI unit of capacitance is ___ (F).
One Farad is equivalent to $1\ C\ V^{-1}$.
The farad is named in honor of which scientist?
$1$ microfarad ($\mu F$) is equal to ___ $F$.
$1$ picofarad ($pF$) is equal to ___ $F$.
When the charge on the capacitor is increased, the capacitance of a capacitor remains constant.
Capacitance of a capacitor depends on which of the following?
a: The size and shape of the capacitor

q: The capacitance of a capacitor depends on the nature of the insulator between the two conductors.
The amount of charge given to a conductor to change its potential by one unit is called the ___ of the conductor.
Typical devices have capacitances ranging from microfarad ($\mu F$) to picofarad ($pF$).
If potential difference $V$ increases while $C$ is constant, what happens to $Q$?
The relationship for capacitance is represented as $C = \frac{Q}{V}$.
$1$ nanofarad ($nF$) = ___ $F$.
What happens to the potential difference when the charge on the capacitor is increased?
A capacitor is an electrical power source.
The potential difference $V$ between two conductors of the capacitor is called the potential difference of the ___.
